[#udb:doc:inst:rv32:vfmv_f_s]
=== pass:[<a id="insn:vfmv.f.s"></a>]vfmv.f.s

Synopsis::
Vector floating-point to scalar move

Base::

[cols="1,1"]
|===
| RV32 | RV64

| &#x2713;
| &#x2713;
|===

Assembly::
vfmv.f.s fd, vs2

Encoding::
[wavedrom, ,svg,subs='attributes',width="100%"]
....
{"reg":[{"bits":7,"name": 0x57,"type":2},{"bits":5,"name": "fd","type":4},{"bits":8,"name": 0x1,"type":2},{"bits":5,"name": "vs2","type":4},{"bits":7,"name": 0x21,"type":2}]}
....

Description::
Moves floating-point element from vector register vs2 to scalar floating-point register fd, using vector mask vm. This operation extracts a single element from the vector.


Decode Variables::
[width="100%", cols="1,2", options="header"]
|===
|Variable Name |Location
|vs2 |$encoding[24:20]
|fd |$encoding[11:7]
|===

Included in::


[width="100%",cols="1,2",options="header"]
|===
| Extension | Version

| *Zvl32b*
| any
|===




[#udb:doc:inst:rv32:vfmv_s_f]
=== pass:[<a id="insn:vfmv.s.f"></a>]vfmv.s.f

Synopsis::
Vector scalar to floating-point move

Base::

[cols="1,1"]
|===
| RV32 | RV64

| &#x2713;
| &#x2713;
|===

Assembly::
vfmv.s.f vd, fs1

Encoding::
[wavedrom, ,svg,subs='attributes',width="100%"]
....
{"reg":[{"bits":7,"name": 0x57,"type":2},{"bits":5,"name": "vd","type":4},{"bits":3,"name": 0x5,"type":2},{"bits":5,"name": "fs1","type":4},{"bits":12,"name": 0x420,"type":2}]}
....

Description::
Moves scalar floating-point value from register fs1 to all elements of vector register vd, using vector mask vm. This operation broadcasts a scalar to a vector.


Decode Variables::

|vd |$encoding[11:7]
|===

| Extension | Version

| *Zvl32b*
| any
|===




[#udb:doc:inst:rv32:vfmv_v_f]
=== pass:[<a id="insn:vfmv.v.f"></a>]vfmv.v.f

Synopsis::
Vector floating-point to vector move

Base::

[cols="1,1"]
|===
| RV32 | RV64

| &#x2713;
| &#x2713;
|===

Assembly::
vfmv.v.f vd, fs1

Encoding::
[wavedrom, ,svg,subs='attributes',width="100%"]
....
{"reg":[{"bits":7,"name": 0x57,"type":2},{"bits":5,"name": "vd","type":4},{"bits":3,"name": 0x5,"type":2},{"bits":5,"name": "fs1","type":4},{"bits":12,"name": 0x5e0,"type":2}]}
....

Description::
Moves floating-point element from scalar register fs1 to vector register vd, using vector mask vm. This operation creates a vector with a single floating-point value.

[width="100%", cols="1,2", options="header"]

[#udb:doc:inst:rv32:vfncvtbf16_f_f_w]
=== pass:[<a id="insn:vfncvtbf16.f.f.w"></a>]vfncvtbf16.f.f.w

Synopsis::
Vector narrowing single-precision to BFloat16 conversion

Base::

[cols="1,1"]
|===
| RV32 | RV64

| &#x2713;
| &#x2713;
|===

Assembly::
vfncvtbf16.f.f.w vd, vs2, vm

Encoding::
[wavedrom, ,svg,subs='attributes',width="100%"]
....
{"reg":[{"bits":7,"name": 0x57,"type":2},{"bits":5,"name": "vd","type":4},{"bits":8,"name": 0xe9,"type":2},{"bits":5,"name": "vs2","type":4},{"bits":1,"name": "vm","type":4},{"bits":6,"name": 0x12,"type":2}]}
....

Description::
Converts each single-precision floating-point element in vector register vs2 to BFloat16 format, storing results in vector register vd according to mask vm. This is a narrowing conversion that compresses 32-bit single-precision values to 16-bit BFloat16 values.


Decode Variables::
[width="100%", cols="1,2", options="header"]
|===
|Variable Name |Location
|vm |$encoding[25]
|vs2 |$encoding[24:20]

|===

|===

[#udb:doc:inst:rv32:vfsub_vf]
=== pass:[<a id="insn:vfsub.vf"></a>]vfsub.vf

Synopsis::
Vector floating-point subtract scalar

Base::

[cols="1,1"]
|===
| RV32 | RV64

| &#x2713;
| &#x2713;
|===

Assembly::
vfsub.vf vd, vs2, fs1, vm

Encoding::
[wavedrom, ,svg,subs='attributes',width="100%"]
....
{"reg":[{"bits":7,"name": 0x57,"type":2},{"bits":5,"name": "vd","type":4},{"bits":3,"name": 0x5,"type":2},{"bits":5,"name": "fs1","type":4},{"bits":5,"name": "vs2","type":4},{"bits":1,"name": "vm","type":4},{"bits":6,"name": 0x2,"type":2}]}
....

Description::
Performs floating-point subtraction on elements from vs2 and scalar from rs1, according to mask vm, and stores results in vd. Element width is determined by SEW setting. Follows IEEE-754 floating-point standards. Supports vector masking and follows vector tail policy.


Decode Variables::
[width="100%", cols="1,2", options="header"]
|===
|Variable Name |Location
|vm |$encoding[25]
|vs2 |$encoding[24:20]
|fs1 |$encoding[19:15]
|vd |$encoding[11:7]
|===

Included in::


[width="100%",cols="1,2",options="header"]
|===
| Extension | Version

| *Zvl32b*
| any
|===




[#udb:doc:inst:rv32:vfsub_vv]
=== pass:[<a id="insn:vfsub.vv"></a>]vfsub.vv

Synopsis::
Vector floating-point subtract

Base::

[cols="1,1"]
|===
| RV32 | RV64

| &#x2713;
| &#x2713;
|===

Assembly::
vfsub.vv vd, vs2, vs1, vm

Encoding::
[wavedrom, ,svg,subs='attributes',width="100%"]
....
{"reg":[{"bits":7,"name": 0x57,"type":2},{"bits":5,"name": "vd","type":4},{"bits":3,"name": 0x1,"type":2},{"bits":5,"name": "vs1","type":4},{"bits":5,"name": "vs2","type":4},{"bits":1,"name": "vm","type":4},{"bits":6,"name": 0x2,"type":2}]}
....

Description::
Performs floating-point subtraction on elements from vs2 and vs1, according to mask vm, and stores results in vd. Element width is determined by SEW setting. Follows IEEE-754 floating-point standards. Supports vector masking and follows vector tail policy.


Decode Variables::
[width="100%", cols="1,2", options="header"]
|===
|Variable Name |Location
|vm |$encoding[25]
|vs2 |$encoding[24:20]
|vs1 |$encoding[19:15]
|vd |$encoding[11:7]
|===

[width="100%",cols="1,2",options="header"]
|===
| Extension | Version

| *Zvl32b*
| any
|===




[#udb:doc:inst:rv32:vfwadd_vf]
=== pass:[<a id="insn:vfwadd.vf"></a>]vfwadd.vf

Synopsis::
Vector floating-point widening addition with scalar

Base::

[cols="1,1"]
|===
| RV32 | RV64

| &#x2713;
| &#x2713;
|===

Assembly::
vfwadd.vf vd, vs2, fs1, vm

Encoding::
[wavedrom, ,svg,subs='attributes',width="100%"]
....
{"reg":[{"bits":7,"name": 0x57,"type":2},{"bits":5,"name": "vd","type":4},{"bits":3,"name": 0x5,"type":2},{"bits":5,"name": "fs1","type":4},{"bits":5,"name": "vs2","type":4},{"bits":1,"name": "vm","type":4},{"bits":6,"name": 0x30,"type":2}]}
....

Description::
Performs floating-point addition on elements from vector register vs2 and floating-point scalar register fs1, widens the results, and stores them in vector register vd, using vector mask vm. The operation follows IEEE 754 floating-point arithmetic rules.


Decode Variables::
[width="100%", cols="1,2", options="header"]
|===
|Variable Name |Location
|vm |$encoding[25]
|vs2 |$encoding[24:20]
|fs1 |$encoding[19:15]
|vd |$encoding[11:7]
|===

Included in::


[width="100%",cols="1,2",options="header"]
|===
| Extension | Version

| *Zvl32b*
| any
|===

....

Description::
Performs floating-point addition on elements from vector registers vs2 and vs1, widens the results, and stores them in vector register vd, using vector mask vm. The operation follows IEEE 754 floating-point arithmetic rules.

=== pass:[<a id="insn:vfwcvtbf16.f.f.v"></a>]vfwcvtbf16.f.f.v

Synopsis::
Vector widening BFloat16 to single-precision conversion

Base::

[cols="1,1"]
|===
| RV32 | RV64

| &#x2713;
| &#x2713;
|===

Assembly::
vfwcvtbf16.f.f.v vd, vs2, vm

Encoding::
[wavedrom, ,svg,subs='attributes',width="100%"]
....
{"reg":[{"bits":7,"name": 0x57,"type":2},{"bits":5,"name": "vd","type":4},{"bits":8,"name": 0x69,"type":2},{"bits":5,"name": "vs2","type":4},{"bits":1,"name": "vm","type":4},{"bits":6,"name": 0x12,"type":2}]}
....

Description::
Converts each BFloat16 element in vector register vs2 to single-precision floating-point format, storing results in vector register vd according to mask vm. This is a widening conversion that expands 16-bit BFloat16 values to 32-bit single-precision values.

Synopsis::

Base::

[cols="1,1"]
|===
| RV32 | RV64

| &#x2713;
| &#x2713;
|===

[wavedrom, ,svg,subs='attributes',width="100%"]

....

Description::
Performs a BFloat16 multiply-accumulate operation on each element in vector register vs2 with scalar register rs1, accumulating results in vector register vd according to mask vm. This is a widening operation that produces single-precision results.

[#udb:doc:inst:rv32:vfwsub_vf]
=== pass:[<a id="insn:vfwsub.vf"></a>]vfwsub.vf

Synopsis::
Vector floating-point widening subtraction with scalar

Base::

[cols="1,1"]
|===
| RV32 | RV64

| &#x2713;
| &#x2713;
|===

Assembly::
vfwsub.vf vd, vs2, fs1, vm

Encoding::
[wavedrom, ,svg,subs='attributes',width="100%"]
....
{"reg":[{"bits":7,"name": 0x57,"type":2},{"bits":5,"name": "vd","type":4},{"bits":3,"name": 0x5,"type":2},{"bits":5,"name": "fs1","type":4},{"bits":5,"name": "vs2","type":4},{"bits":1,"name": "vm","type":4},{"bits":6,"name": 0x32,"type":2}]}
....

Description::
Performs floating-point subtraction on elements from vector register vs2 and floating-point scalar register fs1, widens the results, and stores them in vector register vd, using vector mask vm. The operation follows IEEE 754 floating-point arithmetic rules.


Decode Variables::
[width="100%", cols="1,2", options="header"]
|===
|Variable Name |Location
|vm |$encoding[25]
|vs2 |$encoding[24:20]
|fs1 |$encoding[19:15]
|vd |$encoding[11:7]
|===

Included in::


[width="100%",cols="1,2",options="header"]
|===
| Extension | Version

| *Zvl32b*
| any
|===

[#udb:doc:inst:rv32:vghsh_vv]
=== pass:[<a id="insn:vghsh.vv"></a>]vghsh.vv

Synopsis::
Vector Galois field hash

Base::

[cols="1,1"]
|===
| RV32 | RV64

| &#x2713;
| &#x2713;
|===

Assembly::
vghsh.vv vd, vs2, vs1

Encoding::
[wavedrom, ,svg,subs='attributes',width="100%"]
....
{"reg":[{"bits":7,"name": 0x77,"type":2},{"bits":5,"name": "vd","type":4},{"bits":3,"name": 0x2,"type":2},{"bits":5,"name": "vs1","type":4},{"bits":5,"name": "vs2","type":4},{"bits":7,"name": 0x59,"type":2}]}
....

Description::
Performs a Galois field hash operation on vector register vs2 using vector register vs1 as the hash polynomial, storing results in vector register vd according to mask vm. This instruction is used in cryptographic hash functions and error detection algorithms.


Decode Variables::
[width="100%", cols="1,2", options="header"]
|===
|Variable Name |Location
|vs2 |$encoding[24:20]
|vs1 |$encoding[19:15]
|vd |$encoding[11:7]
|===

Included in::


[width="100%",cols="1,2",options="header"]
|===
| Extension | Version

| *Zvkg*
| any
|===

....

Description::
Performs a Galois field multiply operation on vector register vs2 with vector register vs1, storing results in vector register vd according to mask vm. This instruction is used in cryptographic algorithms and error correction codes.

[#udb:doc:inst:rv32:vl1re16_v]
=== pass:[<a id="insn:vl1re16.v"></a>]vl1re16.v

Synopsis::
Vector load 1 element of 16 bits with register stride

Base::

[cols="1,1"]
|===
| RV32 | RV64

| &#x2713;
| &#x2713;
|===

Assembly::
vl1re16.v vd, (xs1)

Encoding::
[wavedrom, ,svg,subs='attributes',width="100%"]
....
{"reg":[{"bits":7,"name": 0x7,"type":2},{"bits":5,"name": "vd","type":4},{"bits":3,"name": 0x5,"type":2},{"bits":5,"name": "xs1","type":4},{"bits":12,"name": 0x28,"type":2}]}
....

Description::
Loads 1 segment of 16-bit elements from memory starting at address in xs1 register with stride in xs2, using vector mask vm, and stores results in vector register vd. The effective element width is 16 bits.


Decode Variables::
[width="100%", cols="1,2", options="header"]
|===
|Variable Name |Location
|xs1 |$encoding[19:15]
|vd |$encoding[11:7]
|===

Included in::


[width="100%",cols="1,2",options="header"]
|===
| Extension | Version

| *Zvl32b*
| any
|===

=== pass:[<a id="insn:vl2re64.v"></a>]vl2re64.v

Synopsis::
Vector load 2 elements of 64 bits with register stride

Base::

[cols="1,1"]
|===
| RV32 | RV64

| &#x2713;
| &#x2713;
|===

Assembly::
vl2re64.v vd, (xs1)

Encoding::
[wavedrom, ,svg,subs='attributes',width="100%"]
....
{"reg":[{"bits":7,"name": 0x7,"type":2},{"bits":5,"name": "vd","type":4},{"bits":3,"name": 0x7,"type":2},{"bits":5,"name": "xs1","type":4},{"bits":12,"name": 0x228,"type":2}]}
....

Description::
Loads 2 segments of 64-bit elements from memory starting at address in xs1 register with stride in xs2, using vector mask vm, and stores results in vector register vd. The effective element width is 64 bits.

[#udb:doc:inst:rv32:vlse8_v]
=== pass:[<a id="insn:vlse8.v"></a>]vlse8.v

Synopsis::
Vector load 8-bit elements with stride

Base::

[cols="1,1"]
|===
| RV32 | RV64

| &#x2713;
| &#x2713;
|===

Assembly::
vlse8.v vd, (xs1), xs2, vm

Encoding::
[wavedrom, ,svg,subs='attributes',width="100%"]
....
{"reg":[{"bits":7,"name": 0x7,"type":2},{"bits":5,"name": "vd","type":4},{"bits":3,"name": 0x0,"type":2},{"bits":5,"name": "xs1","type":4},{"bits":5,"name": "xs2","type":4},{"bits":1,"name": "vm","type":4},{"bits":6,"name": 0x2,"type":2}]}
....

Description::
Loads 8-bit elements from memory starting at address in xs1 register with stride in xs2, using vector mask vm, and stores results in vector register vd. The effective element width is 8 bits.


Decode Variables::
[width="100%", cols="1,2", options="header"]
|===
|Variable Name |Location
|vm |$encoding[25]
|xs2 |$encoding[24:20]
|xs1 |$encoding[19:15]
|vd |$encoding[11:7]
|===

Included in::


[width="100%",cols="1,2",options="header"]
|===
| Extension | Version

| *Zvl32b*
| any
|===

Synopsis::
Vector segmented load 4 elements of 16 bits, fault-first

Base::

[cols="1,1"]
|===
| RV32 | RV64

| &#x2713;
| &#x2713;
|===

Assembly::
vlseg4e16ff.v vd, (xs1), vm

Encoding::
[wavedrom, ,svg,subs='attributes',width="100%"]
....
{"reg":[{"bits":7,"name": 0x7,"type":2},{"bits":5,"name": "vd","type":4},{"bits":3,"name": 0x5,"type":2},{"bits":5,"name": "xs1","type":4},{"bits":5,"name": 0x10,"type":2},{"bits":1,"name": "vm","type":4},{"bits":6,"name": 0x18,"type":2}]}
....

Description::
Loads 4 segments of 16-bit elements from memory starting at address in xs1 register, using vector mask vm, and stores results in vector register vd. This is a fault-first operation that stops on the first fault. The effective element width is 16 bits.

....

Description::
Loads 8 segments of 64-bit elements from memory starting at address in xs1 register, using vector mask vm, and stores results in vector register vd. The effective element width is 64 bits regardless of SEW setting.

[#udb:doc:inst:rv32:vmadc_vi]
=== pass:[<a id="insn:vmadc.vi"></a>]vmadc.vi

Synopsis::
Vector multiply-add with carry and immediate

Base::

[cols="1,1"]
|===
| RV32 | RV64

| &#x2713;
| &#x2713;
|===

Assembly::
vmadc.vi vd, vs2, imm

Encoding::
[wavedrom, ,svg,subs='attributes',width="100%"]
....
{"reg":[{"bits":7,"name": 0x57,"type":2},{"bits":5,"name": "vd","type":4},{"bits":3,"name": 0x3,"type":2},{"bits":5,"name": "imm","type":4},{"bits":5,"name": "vs2","type":4},{"bits":7,"name": 0x23,"type":2}]}
....

Description::
Performs multiply-add operation on elements from vector register vs2 and immediate value, adds the carry-in from vector register vs3, and stores results in vector register vd, using vector mask vm.


Decode Variables::
[width="100%", cols="1,2", options="header"]
|===
|Variable Name |Location
|vs2 |$encoding[24:20]
|imm |sext($encoding[19:15], 5)
|vd |$encoding[11:7]
|===

Included in::


[width="100%",cols="1,2",options="header"]
|===
| Extension | Version

| *Zvl32b*
| any
|===

....

Performs multiply-add operation on elements from vector register vs2 and immediate value, adds the carry-in from vector register vs3, and stores results in vector register vd, using vector mask vm. This variant includes additional masking behavior.

{"reg":[{"bits":7,"name": 0x57,"type":2},{"bits":5,"name": "vd","type":4},{"bits":3,"name": 0x4,"type":2},{"bits":5,"name": "xs1","type":4},{"bits":5,"name": "vs2","type":4},{"bits":7,"name": 0x23,"type":2}]}
....

Description::
Performs multiply-add operation on elements from vector register vs2 and scalar register xs1, adds the carry-in from vector register vs3, and stores results in vector register vd, using vector mask vm.

....

Description::
Performs multiply-add operation on elements from vector register vs2 and scalar register xs1, adds the carry-in from vector register vs3, and stores results in vector register vd, using vector mask vm. This variant includes additional masking behavior.

[#udb:doc:inst:rv32:vmerge_vvm]
=== pass:[<a id="insn:vmerge.vvm"></a>]vmerge.vvm

Synopsis::
Vector merge with mask

Base::

[cols="1,1"]
|===
| RV32 | RV64

| &#x2713;
| &#x2713;
|===

Assembly::
vmerge.vvm vd, vs2, vs1, v0

Encoding::
[wavedrom, ,svg,subs='attributes',width="100%"]
....
{"reg":[{"bits":7,"name": 0x57,"type":2},{"bits":5,"name": "vd","type":4},{"bits":3,"name": 0x0,"type":2},{"bits":5,"name": "vs1","type":4},{"bits":5,"name": "vs2","type":4},{"bits":7,"name": 0x2e,"type":2}]}
....

Description::
Merges elements from vs1 and vs2 using mask register v0 and stores results in vd. Where mask bits are set, elements from vs1 are selected; otherwise elements from vs2 are selected.


Decode Variables::
[width="100%", cols="1,2", options="header"]
|===
|Variable Name |Location
|vs2 |$encoding[24:20]
|vs1 |$encoding[19:15]
|vd |$encoding[11:7]
|===

[width="100%",cols="1,2",options="header"]
|===
| Extension | Version

| *Zvl32b*
| any
|===

[#udb:doc:inst:rv32:vmsbc_vv]
=== pass:[<a id="insn:vmsbc.vv"></a>]vmsbc.vv

Synopsis::
Vector multiply-subtract with carry

Base::

[cols="1,1"]
|===
| RV32 | RV64

| &#x2713;
| &#x2713;
|===

Assembly::
vmsbc.vv vd, vs2, vs1

Encoding::
[wavedrom, ,svg,subs='attributes',width="100%"]
....
{"reg":[{"bits":7,"name": 0x57,"type":2},{"bits":5,"name": "vd","type":4},{"bits":3,"name": 0x0,"type":2},{"bits":5,"name": "vs1","type":4},{"bits":5,"name": "vs2","type":4},{"bits":7,"name": 0x27,"type":2}]}
....

Description::
Performs multiply-subtract operation on elements from vector registers vs2 and vs1, subtracts the carry-in from vector register vs3, and stores results in vector register vd, using vector mask vm.


Decode Variables::
[width="100%", cols="1,2", options="header"]
|===
|Variable Name |Location
|vs2 |$encoding[24:20]
|vs1 |$encoding[19:15]
|vd |$encoding[11:7]
|===

Included in::


[width="100%",cols="1,2",options="header"]
|===
| Extension | Version

| *Zvl32b*
| any
|===

[#udb:doc:inst:rv32:vmseq_vi]
=== pass:[<a id="insn:vmseq.vi"></a>]vmseq.vi

Synopsis::
Vector mask equal immediate

Base::

[cols="1,1"]
|===
| RV32 | RV64

| &#x2713;
| &#x2713;
|===

Assembly::
vmseq.vi vd, vs2, imm, vm

Encoding::
[wavedrom, ,svg,subs='attributes',width="100%"]
....
{"reg":[{"bits":7,"name": 0x57,"type":2},{"bits":5,"name": "vd","type":4},{"bits":3,"name": 0x3,"type":2},{"bits":5,"name": "imm","type":4},{"bits":5,"name": "vs2","type":4},{"bits":1,"name": "vm","type":4},{"bits":6,"name": 0x18,"type":2}]}
....

Description::
Sets mask bits where elements in vs2 equal immediate value, according to mask vm, and stores results in vd. Element width is determined by SEW setting. Supports vector masking and follows vector tail policy.


Decode Variables::
[width="100%", cols="1,2", options="header"]
|===
|Variable Name |Location
|vm |$encoding[25]
|vs2 |$encoding[24:20]
|imm |sext($encoding[19:15], 5)
|vd |$encoding[11:7]
|===

Included in::


[width="100%",cols="1,2",options="header"]
|===
| Extension | Version

| *Zvl32b*
| any
|===

[wavedrom, ,svg,subs='attributes',width="100%"]

{"reg":[{"bits":7,"name": 0x57,"type":2},{"bits":5,"name": "vd","type":4},{"bits":3,"name": 0x4,"type":2},{"bits":5,"name": "xs1","type":4},{"bits":5,"name": "vs2","type":4},{"bits":1,"name": "vm","type":4},{"bits":6,"name": 0x1f,"type":2}]}
....

Description::
Sets mask bits where elements in vs2 are greater than scalar in rs1 (signed), according to mask vm, and stores results in vd. Element width is determined by SEW setting. Supports vector masking and follows vector tail policy.

[#udb:doc:inst:rv32:vmv_v_i]
=== pass:[<a id="insn:vmv.v.i"></a>]vmv.v.i

Synopsis::
Vector move immediate to vector register

Base::

[cols="1,1"]
|===
| RV32 | RV64

| &#x2713;
| &#x2713;
|===

Assembly::
vmv.v.i vd, imm

Encoding::
[wavedrom, ,svg,subs='attributes',width="100%"]
....
{"reg":[{"bits":7,"name": 0x57,"type":2},{"bits":5,"name": "vd","type":4},{"bits":3,"name": 0x3,"type":2},{"bits":5,"name": "imm","type":4},{"bits":12,"name": 0x5e0,"type":2}]}
....

Description::
Copies a sign-extended 5-bit immediate value to all body elements of the destination
vector register group. This is an unmasked instruction (vm=1).

The 5-bit immediate (simm[4:0]) is sign-extended to SEW bits and written to each
body element of the destination vector register group (elements from vstart to vl-1).
Prestart elements (indices less than vstart) are unchanged.
Tail elements (indices >= vl) are handled according to the tail agnostic/undisturbed policy.


Decode Variables::
[width="100%", cols="1,2", options="header"]
|===
|Variable Name |Location
|imm |$encoding[19:15]
|vd |$encoding[11:7]
|===

Included in::


[width="100%",cols="1,2",options="header"]
|===
| Extension | Version

| *Zvl32b*
| any
|===

=== pass:[<a id="insn:vmv.v.v"></a>]vmv.v.v

Synopsis::
Vector register move

Base::

[cols="1,1"]
|===
| RV32 | RV64

| &#x2713;
| &#x2713;
|===

Assembly::

Encoding::
[wavedrom, ,svg,subs='attributes',width="100%"]
....
{"reg":[{"bits":7,"name": 0x57,"type":2},{"bits":5,"name": "vd","type":4},{"bits":3,"name": 0x0,"type":2},{"bits":5,"name": "vs1","type":4},{"bits":12,"name": 0x5e0,"type":2}]}
....

Description::
Copies elements from vector register vs2 to vector register vd, using vector mask vm. This operation performs a simple register-to-register copy.


Decode Variables::
[width="100%", cols="1,2", options="header"]
|===
|Variable Name |Location
|vs1 |$encoding[19:15]
|vd |$encoding[11:7]
|===

Included in::


[width="100%",cols="1,2",options="header"]
|===
| Extension | Version

| *Zvl32b*
| any
|===

=== pass:[<a id="insn:vmv.v.x"></a>]vmv.v.x

Synopsis::
Vector scalar to vector move

Base::

[cols="1,1"]
|===
| RV32 | RV64

| &#x2713;
| &#x2713;
|===

Assembly::
vmv.v.x vd, xs1

Encoding::
[wavedrom, ,svg,subs='attributes',width="100%"]
....
{"reg":[{"bits":7,"name": 0x57,"type":2},{"bits":5,"name": "vd","type":4},{"bits":3,"name": 0x4,"type":2},{"bits":5,"name": "xs1","type":4},{"bits":12,"name": 0x5e0,"type":2}]}
....

Description::
Broadcasts scalar register xs1 to all elements of vector register vd, using vector mask vm. This operation creates a vector with identical elements.

[width="100%", cols="1,2", options="header"]

[#udb:doc:inst:rv32:vmv_x_s]
=== pass:[<a id="insn:vmv.x.s"></a>]vmv.x.s

Synopsis::
Vector move scalar to integer register

Base::

[cols="1,1"]
|===
| RV32 | RV64

| &#x2713;
| &#x2713;
|===

Assembly::
vmv.x.s xd, vs2

Encoding::
[wavedrom, ,svg,subs='attributes',width="100%"]
....
{"reg":[{"bits":7,"name": 0x57,"type":2},{"bits":5,"name": "xd","type":4},{"bits":8,"name": 0x2,"type":2},{"bits":5,"name": "vs2","type":4},{"bits":7,"name": 0x21,"type":2}]}
....

Description::
Copies the first element of the source vector register vs2 to the
destination integer register xd, sign-extending to XLEN bits if SEW < XLEN.

This is a scalar read instruction that always reads element 0 of vs2,
regardless of vstart. The vstart CSR is reset to 0 after execution.


Decode Variables::
[width="100%", cols="1,2", options="header"]
|===
|Variable Name |Location
|vs2 |$encoding[24:20]
|xd |$encoding[11:7]
|===

[width="100%",cols="1,2",options="header"]
|===
| Extension | Version

| *Zvl32b*
| any
|===

[#udb:doc:inst:rv32:vnclip_wi]
=== pass:[<a id="insn:vnclip.wi"></a>]vnclip.wi

Synopsis::
Vector widening signed clip with immediate

Base::

[cols="1,1"]
|===
| RV32 | RV64

| &#x2713;
| &#x2713;
|===

Assembly::
vnclip.wi vd, vs2, imm, vm

Encoding::
[wavedrom, ,svg,subs='attributes',width="100%"]
....
{"reg":[{"bits":7,"name": 0x57,"type":2},{"bits":5,"name": "vd","type":4},{"bits":3,"name": 0x3,"type":2},{"bits":5,"name": "imm","type":4},{"bits":5,"name": "vs2","type":4},{"bits":1,"name": "vm","type":4},{"bits":6,"name": 0x2f,"type":2}]}
....

Description::
Performs widening signed clip operation on elements from vector register vs2 using immediate clip value and stores results in vector register vd, using vector mask vm. The operation clips values to a specified range and widens the result.


Decode Variables::
[width="100%", cols="1,2", options="header"]
|===
|Variable Name |Location
|vm |$encoding[25]
|vs2 |$encoding[24:20]
|imm |$encoding[19:15]

|===

|===
| Extension | Version

| *Zvl32b*
| any
|===




[#udb:doc:inst:rv32:vnclip_wv]
=== pass:[<a id="insn:vnclip.wv"></a>]vnclip.wv

Synopsis::
Vector widening signed clip

Base::

[cols="1,1"]
|===
| RV32 | RV64

| &#x2713;
| &#x2713;
|===

Assembly::
vnclip.wv vd, vs2, vs1, vm

Encoding::
[wavedrom, ,svg,subs='attributes',width="100%"]
....
{"reg":[{"bits":7,"name": 0x57,"type":2},{"bits":5,"name": "vd","type":4},{"bits":3,"name": 0x0,"type":2},{"bits":5,"name": "vs1","type":4},{"bits":5,"name": "vs2","type":4},{"bits":1,"name": "vm","type":4},{"bits":6,"name": 0x2f,"type":2}]}
....

Description::
Performs widening signed clip operation on elements from vector register vs2 using clip values from vector register vs1 and stores results in vector register vd, using vector mask vm. The operation clips values to a specified range and widens the result.


Decode Variables::
[width="100%", cols="1,2", options="header"]
|===

Synopsis::
Vector widening signed clip with scalar

Base::

[cols="1,1"]
|===
| RV32 | RV64

| &#x2713;
| &#x2713;
|===

Assembly::
vnclip.wx vd, vs2, xs1, vm

Encoding::
[wavedrom, ,svg,subs='attributes',width="100%"]
....
{"reg":[{"bits":7,"name": 0x57,"type":2},{"bits":5,"name": "vd","type":4},{"bits":3,"name": 0x4,"type":2},{"bits":5,"name": "xs1","type":4},{"bits":5,"name": "vs2","type":4},{"bits":1,"name": "vm","type":4},{"bits":6,"name": 0x2f,"type":2}]}
....

Description::
Performs widening signed clip operation on elements from vector register vs2 using scalar clip value from register xs1 and stores results in vector register vd, using vector mask vm. The operation clips values to a specified range and widens the result.

[#udb:doc:inst:rv32:vnsra_wi]
=== pass:[<a id="insn:vnsra.wi"></a>]vnsra.wi

Synopsis::
Vector widening signed shift right arithmetic with immediate

Base::

[cols="1,1"]
|===
| RV32 | RV64

| &#x2713;
| &#x2713;
|===

Assembly::
vnsra.wi vd, vs2, imm, vm

Encoding::
[wavedrom, ,svg,subs='attributes',width="100%"]
....
{"reg":[{"bits":7,"name": 0x57,"type":2},{"bits":5,"name": "vd","type":4},{"bits":3,"name": 0x3,"type":2},{"bits":5,"name": "imm","type":4},{"bits":5,"name": "vs2","type":4},{"bits":1,"name": "vm","type":4},{"bits":6,"name": 0x2d,"type":2}]}
....

Description::
Performs widening signed arithmetic right shift on elements from vector register vs2 by immediate shift amount and stores results in vector register vd, using vector mask vm. The operation preserves the sign bit and widens the result.

| &#x2713;

Synopsis::

[cols="1,1"]
|===
| RV32 | RV64

| &#x2713;
| &#x2713;
|===

Assembly::

[wavedrom, ,svg,subs='attributes',width="100%"]
....
{"reg":[{"bits":7,"name": 0x57,"type":2},{"bits":5,"name": "vd","type":4},{"bits":8,"name": 0x4a,"type":2},{"bits":5,"name": "vs2","type":4},{"bits":1,"name": "vm","type":4},{"bits":6,"name": 0x12,"type":2}]}
....

Description::
Reverses the byte order within each element of vector register vs2, storing results in vector register vd according to mask vm. This is equivalent to a byte-level reversal of each element.

....

Description::
Gathers elements from vector register vs2 using immediate index value and stores results in vector register vd, using vector mask vm. This operation performs indexed element selection.

[#udb:doc:inst:rv32:vrol_vv]
=== pass:[<a id="insn:vrol.vv"></a>]vrol.vv

Synopsis::
Vector rotate left by vector

Base::

[cols="1,1"]
|===
| RV32 | RV64

| &#x2713;
| &#x2713;
|===

Assembly::
vrol.vv vd, vs2, vs1, vm

Encoding::
[wavedrom, ,svg,subs='attributes',width="100%"]
....
{"reg":[{"bits":7,"name": 0x57,"type":2},{"bits":5,"name": "vd","type":4},{"bits":3,"name": 0x0,"type":2},{"bits":5,"name": "vs1","type":4},{"bits":5,"name": "vs2","type":4},{"bits":1,"name": "vm","type":4},{"bits":6,"name": 0x15,"type":2}]}
....

Description::
Rotates each element in vector register vs2 left by the amount specified in vector register vs1, storing results in vector register vd according to mask vm. The rotation amount is taken modulo the element width.

[width="100%", cols="1,2", options="header"]

|vs2 |$encoding[24:20]

|===
| Extension | Version

| any
|===

{"reg":[{"bits":7,"name": 0x57,"type":2},{"bits":5,"name": "vd","type":4},{"bits":3,"name": 0x0,"type":2},{"bits":5,"name": "vs1","type":4},{"bits":5,"name": "vs2","type":4},{"bits":1,"name": "vm","type":4},{"bits":6,"name": 0x14,"type":2}]}
....

Description::
Rotates each element in vector register vs2 right by the amount specified in vector register vs1, storing results in vector register vd according to mask vm. The rotation amount is taken modulo the element width.

{"reg":[{"bits":7,"name": 0x27,"type":2},{"bits":5,"name": "vs3","type":4},{"bits":3,"name": 0x0,"type":2},{"bits":5,"name": "xs1","type":4},{"bits":5,"name": 0x0,"type":2},{"bits":1,"name": "vm","type":4},{"bits":6,"name": 0x0,"type":2}]}
....

Description::
Stores 8-bit elements from vector register vs3 to memory starting at address in xs1 register, using vector mask vm. The effective element width is 8 bits regardless of SEW setting.

[cols="1,1"]
|===
| RV32 | RV64

| &#x2713;
| &#x2713;

[wavedrom, ,svg,subs='attributes',width="100%"]

....

Description::
Sign-extends elements from vector register vs2 by a factor of 2 and stores results in vector register vd, using vector mask vm. The operation extends SEW to 2×SEW.

....

Description::
Performs the SHA-2 choose function (Ch) on vector register vs2 using vector register vs1, storing results in vector register vd according to mask vm. This is used in SHA-256 and SHA-512 hash computations.

[#udb:doc:inst:rv32:vsll_vi]
=== pass:[<a id="insn:vsll.vi"></a>]vsll.vi

Synopsis::
Vector shift left logical immediate

Base::

[cols="1,1"]
|===
| RV32 | RV64

| &#x2713;
| &#x2713;
|===

Assembly::
vsll.vi vd, vs2, imm, vm

Encoding::
[wavedrom, ,svg,subs='attributes',width="100%"]
....
{"reg":[{"bits":7,"name": 0x57,"type":2},{"bits":5,"name": "vd","type":4},{"bits":3,"name": 0x3,"type":2},{"bits":5,"name": "imm","type":4},{"bits":5,"name": "vs2","type":4},{"bits":1,"name": "vm","type":4},{"bits":6,"name": 0x25,"type":2}]}
....

Description::
Performs logical left shift on elements from vs2 using immediate shift amount, according to mask vm, and stores results in vd. Only the low lg2(SEW) bits of the shift amount are used. Supports vector masking and follows vector tail policy.

[width="100%", cols="1,2", options="header"]

| RV32 | RV64

| &#x2713;
| &#x2713;

Encoding::
[wavedrom, ,svg,subs='attributes',width="100%"]
....

....

[wavedrom, ,svg,subs='attributes',width="100%"]
....

....

Description::
Performs SM3 compression operation on vector register vs2 using immediate value as round constant, storing results in vector register vd according to mask vm. This is used in the SM3 hash function compression phase.

[#udb:doc:inst:rv32:vsra_vi]
=== pass:[<a id="insn:vsra.vi"></a>]vsra.vi

Synopsis::
Vector shift right arithmetic immediate

Base::

[cols="1,1"]
|===
| RV32 | RV64

| &#x2713;
| &#x2713;
|===

Assembly::
vsra.vi vd, vs2, imm, vm

Encoding::
[wavedrom, ,svg,subs='attributes',width="100%"]
....
{"reg":[{"bits":7,"name": 0x57,"type":2},{"bits":5,"name": "vd","type":4},{"bits":3,"name": 0x3,"type":2},{"bits":5,"name": "imm","type":4},{"bits":5,"name": "vs2","type":4},{"bits":1,"name": "vm","type":4},{"bits":6,"name": 0x29,"type":2}]}
....

Description::
Performs arithmetic right shift on elements from vs2 using immediate shift amount, according to mask vm, and stores results in vd. Only the low lg2(SEW) bits of the shift amount are used. Supports vector masking and follows vector tail policy.


Decode Variables::
[width="100%", cols="1,2", options="header"]
|===
|Variable Name |Location
|vm |$encoding[25]
|vs2 |$encoding[24:20]
|imm |$encoding[19:15]
|vd |$encoding[11:7]
|===

Included in::


[width="100%",cols="1,2",options="header"]
|===
| Extension | Version

| *Zvl32b*
| any
|===

[cols="1,1"]
|===
| RV32 | RV64

| &#x2713;
| &#x2713;
|===

Assembly::
vsseg2e8.v vs3, (xs1), vm

Encoding::
[wavedrom, ,svg,subs='attributes',width="100%"]
....
{"reg":[{"bits":7,"name": 0x27,"type":2},{"bits":5,"name": "vs3","type":4},{"bits":3,"name": 0x0,"type":2},{"bits":5,"name": "xs1","type":4},{"bits":5,"name": 0x0,"type":2},{"bits":1,"name": "vm","type":4},{"bits":6,"name": 0x8,"type":2}]}
....

Description::
Stores 2 segments of 8-bit elements from vector register vs2 to memory starting at address in xs1 register, using vector mask vm. The effective element width is 8 bits regardless of SEW setting.

|vm |$encoding[25]
|xs1 |$encoding[19:15]

Stores 7 segments of 32-bit elements from vector register vs2 to memory starting at address in xs1 register, using vector mask vm. The effective element width is 32 bits regardless of SEW setting.

[#udb:doc:inst:rv32:vssra_vi]
=== pass:[<a id="insn:vssra.vi"></a>]vssra.vi

Synopsis::
Vector signed shift right arithmetic with immediate

Base::

[cols="1,1"]
|===
| RV32 | RV64

| &#x2713;
| &#x2713;
|===

Assembly::
vssra.vi vd, vs2, imm, vm

Encoding::
[wavedrom, ,svg,subs='attributes',width="100%"]
....
{"reg":[{"bits":7,"name": 0x57,"type":2},{"bits":5,"name": "vd","type":4},{"bits":3,"name": 0x3,"type":2},{"bits":5,"name": "imm","type":4},{"bits":5,"name": "vs2","type":4},{"bits":1,"name": "vm","type":4},{"bits":6,"name": 0x2b,"type":2}]}
....

Description::
Performs signed arithmetic right shift on elements from vector register vs2 by immediate shift amount and stores results in vector register vd, using vector mask vm. The operation preserves the sign bit.

[width="100%", cols="1,2", options="header"]
|===
|Variable Name |Location
|vm |$encoding[25]
|vs2 |$encoding[24:20]
|imm |$encoding[19:15]
|vd |$encoding[11:7]
|===

[width="100%",cols="1,2",options="header"]
|===
| Extension | Version

| *Zvl32b*
| any
|===

[#udb:doc:inst:rv32:vssseg7e8_v]
=== pass:[<a id="insn:vssseg7e8.v"></a>]vssseg7e8.v

Synopsis::
Vector segmented store 7 elements of 8 bits with stride

Base::

[cols="1,1"]
|===
| RV32 | RV64

| &#x2713;
| &#x2713;
|===

Assembly::
vssseg7e8.v vs3, (xs1), xs2, vm

Encoding::
[wavedrom, ,svg,subs='attributes',width="100%"]
....
{"reg":[{"bits":7,"name": 0x27,"type":2},{"bits":5,"name": "vs3","type":4},{"bits":3,"name": 0x0,"type":2},{"bits":5,"name": "xs1","type":4},{"bits":5,"name": "xs2","type":4},{"bits":1,"name": "vm","type":4},{"bits":6,"name": 0x32,"type":2}]}
....

Description::
Stores 7 segments of 8-bit elements from vector register vs2 to memory starting at address in xs1 register with stride, using vector mask vm. The effective element width is 8 bits.

[width="100%", cols="1,2", options="header"]
|===
|Variable Name |Location
|vm |$encoding[25]
|xs2 |$encoding[24:20]
|xs1 |$encoding[19:15]
|vs3 |$encoding[11:7]
|===

....

Description::
Stores 7 segments of 16-bit elements from vector register vs2 to memory using unordered access pattern with index register vs1, starting at address in xs1 register, using vector mask vm. The effective element width is 16 bits.

=== pass:[<a id="insn:vwadd.vv"></a>]vwadd.vv

Synopsis::
Vector widening unsigned addition

Base::

[cols="1,1"]
|===
| RV32 | RV64

| &#x2713;
| &#x2713;
|===

Assembly::
vwadd.vv vd, vs2, vs1, vm

Encoding::
[wavedrom, ,svg,subs='attributes',width="100%"]
....
{"reg":[{"bits":7,"name": 0x57,"type":2},{"bits":5,"name": "vd","type":4},{"bits":3,"name": 0x2,"type":2},{"bits":5,"name": "vs1","type":4},{"bits":5,"name": "vs2","type":4},{"bits":1,"name": "vm","type":4},{"bits":6,"name": 0x31,"type":2}]}
....

Description::
Performs unsigned addition on elements from vector registers vs2 and vs1, widens the results, and stores them in vector register vd, using vector mask vm. The operation follows standard unsigned arithmetic rules.

Base::

[cols="1,1"]
|===
| RV32 | RV64

| &#x2713;
| &#x2713;

Assembly::
vwmaccsu.vv vd, vs1, vs2, vm

Encoding::
[wavedrom, ,svg,subs='attributes',width="100%"]

{"reg":[{"bits":7,"name": 0x57,"type":2},{"bits":5,"name": "vd","type":4},{"bits":3,"name": 0x2,"type":2},{"bits":5,"name": "vs1","type":4},{"bits":5,"name": "vs2","type":4},{"bits":1,"name": "vm","type":4},{"bits":6,"name": 0x3f,"type":2}]}
....

Description::
Performs widening signed-unsigned multiplication on elements from vector registers vs2 (signed) and vs1 (unsigned), adds the result to elements from vector register vd, and stores the final results in vector register vd, using vector mask vm.

[#udb:doc:inst:rv32:wrs_nto]
=== pass:[<a id="insn:wrs.nto"></a>]wrs.nto

Synopsis::
Wait-on-Reservation-Set-with-No-Timeout

Base::

[cols="1,1"]
|===
| RV32 | RV64

| &#x2713;
| &#x2713;
|===

Assembly::
wrs.nto

Encoding::
[wavedrom, ,svg,subs='attributes',width="100%"]
....
{"reg":[{"bits":32,"name": 0xd00073,"type":2}]}
....

Description::
To mitigate the wasteful looping in such usages, a xref:insts:wrs_nto.adoc#udb:doc:inst:wrs_nto[wrs.nto] (WRS-with-no-timeout) instruction is provided.
Instead of polling for a store to a specific memory location, software registers a reservation set that
includes all the bytes of the memory location using the LR instruction. Then a subsequent xref:insts:wrs_nto.adoc#udb:doc:inst:wrs_nto[wrs.nto]
instruction would cause the hart to temporarily stall execution in a low-power state until a store
occurs to the reservation set or an interrupt is observed.

This instruction is not supported in a constrained LR/SC loop.
While stalled, an implementation is permitted to occasionally terminate the stall and complete
execution for any reason.

xref:insts:wrs_nto.adoc#udb:doc:inst:wrs_nto[wrs.nto] follows the rules of the WFI instruction for resuming execution
on a pending interrupt.

When the TW (Timeout Wait) bit in xref:csrs:mstatus.adoc#udb:doc:csr:mstatus[mstatus] is set and xref:insts:wrs_nto.adoc#udb:doc:inst:wrs_nto[wrs.nto] is executed
in any privilege mode otherthan M mode, and it does not complete within an implementation-specific
bounded time limit, the xref:insts:wrs_nto.adoc#udb:doc:inst:wrs_nto[wrs.nto] instruction will cause an illegal instruction exception.

When executing in VS or VU mode, if the VTW bit is set in xref:csrs:hstatus.adoc#udb:doc:csr:hstatus[hstatus], the TW bit in xref:csrs:mstatus.adoc#udb:doc:csr:mstatus[mstatus] is clear,
and the xref:insts:wrs_nto.adoc#udb:doc:inst:wrs_nto[wrs.nto] does not complete within an implementation-specific bounded time limit,
the xref:insts:wrs_nto.adoc#udb:doc:inst:wrs_nto[wrs.nto] instruction will cause a virtual instruction exception.

[Note]
Since xref:insts:wrs_nto.adoc#udb:doc:inst:wrs_nto[wrs.nto] can complete execution for reasons other than stores to the reservation set,
software will likely need a means of looping until the required stores have occurred.

[Note]
xref:insts:wrs_nto.adoc#udb:doc:inst:wrs_nto[wrs.nto], unlike WFI, is not specified to cause an illegal instruction exception if executed in U-mode
when the governing TW bit is 0. WFI is typically not expected to be used in U-mode and on many systems
may promptly cause an illegal instruction exception if used at U-mode.
Unlike WFI, xref:insts:wrs_nto.adoc#udb:doc:inst:wrs_nto[wrs.nto] is expected to be used by software in U-mode when waiting on memory but without
a deadline for that wait.


Decode Variables::
wrs.nto has no decode variables.

Included in::


[width="100%",cols="1,2",options="header"]
|===
| Extension | Version

| *Zawrs*
| any
|===




[#udb:doc:inst:rv32:wrs_sto]
=== pass:[<a id="insn:wrs.sto"></a>]wrs.sto

Synopsis::
Wait-on-Reservation-Set-with-Short-Timeout

Base::

[cols="1,1"]
|===
| RV32 | RV64

| &#x2713;
| &#x2713;
|===

Assembly::
wrs.sto

Encoding::
[wavedrom, ,svg,subs='attributes',width="100%"]
....
{"reg":[{"bits":32,"name": 0x1d00073,"type":2}]}
....

Description::
Instead of polling for a store to a specific memory location, software registers a
reservation set that includes all the bytes of the memory location using the LR instruction.
A subsequent xref:insts:wrs_sto.adoc#udb:doc:inst:wrs_sto[wrs.sto] instruction would cause the hart to temporarily stall execution in a
low-power state until a store occurs to the reservation set or an interrupt is observed.
Sometimes the program waiting on a memory update may also need to carry out a task at a future time
or otherwise place an upper bound on the wait. To support such use cases, xref:insts:wrs_sto.adoc#udb:doc:inst:wrs_sto[wrs.sto] bounds the
stall duration to an implementation-define short timeout such that the stall is terminated on the
timeout if no other conditions have occurred to terminate the stall. The program using this instruction
may then determine if its deadline has been reached.
xref:insts:wrs_sto.adoc#udb:doc:inst:wrs_sto[wrs.sto] causes the hart to temporarily stall execution in a low-power state as long as the reservation
set is valid and no pending interrupts, even if disabled, are observed. For xref:insts:wrs_sto.adoc#udb:doc:inst:wrs_sto[wrs.sto] the stall duration
is bounded by an implementation defined short timeout. These instructions are not supported in a
constrained LR/SC loop.
Hart execution may be stalled while the following conditions are all satisfied:
a. The reservation set is valid
b. If xref:insts:wrs_sto.adoc#udb:doc:inst:wrs_sto[wrs.sto], a "short" duration since start of stall has not elapsed
c. No pending interrupt is observed (see the rules below)

While stalled, an implementation is permitted to occasionally terminate the stall and complete
execution for any reason. xref:insts:wrs_sto.adoc#udb:doc:inst:wrs_sto[wrs.sto] follows the rules of the WFI instruction for resuming execution
on a pending interrupt. Since xref:insts:wrs_sto.adoc#udb:doc:inst:wrs_sto[wrs.sto] can complete execution for reasons other than stores to
the reservation set, software will likely need a means of looping until the required stores have occurred.

[Note]
The duration of a xref:insts:wrs_sto.adoc#udb:doc:inst:wrs_sto[wrs.sto] instruction's timeout may vary significantly within and among implementations.
In typical implementations this duration should be roughly in the range of 10 to 100 times an on-chip
cache miss latency or a cacheless access to main memory.


Decode Variables::
wrs.sto has no decode variables.

Included in::


[width="100%",cols="1,2",options="header"]
|===
| Extension | Version

| *Zawrs*
| any
|===

Synopsis::

Base::

|===
| RV32 | RV64

| &#x2713;
| &#x2713;
|===

Assembly::
xperm4 xd, xs1, xs2

Encoding::
[wavedrom, ,svg,subs='attributes',width="100%"]
....
{"reg":[{"bits":7,"name": 0x33,"type":2},{"bits":5,"name": "xd","type":4},{"bits":3,"name": 0x2,"type":2},{"bits":5,"name": "xs1","type":4},{"bits":5,"name": "xs2","type":4},{"bits":7,"name": 0x14,"type":2}]}
....

Description::
The xperm4 instruction operates on nibbles. The xs1 register contains a vector of XLEN/4 4-bit
elements. The xs2 register contains a vector of XLEN/4 4-bit indexes. The result is each element in
xs2 replaced by the indexed element in xs1, or zero if the index into xs2 is out of bounds.


Decode Variables::
[width="100%", cols="1,2", options="header"]
|===
|Variable Name |Location
|xs2 |$encoding[24:20]
|xs1 |$encoding[19:15]
|xd |$encoding[11:7]
|===

Included in::


[width="100%",cols="1,2",options="header"]
|===
| Extension | Version

| *Zbkx*
| any
|===




[#udb:doc:inst:rv32:xperm8]
=== pass:[<a id="insn:xperm8"></a>]xperm8

Synopsis::
Crossbar permutation (bytes)

Base::

[cols="1,1"]
|===
| RV32 | RV64

| &#x2713;
| &#x2713;
|===

Assembly::
xperm8 xd, xs1, xs2

Encoding::
[wavedrom, ,svg,subs='attributes',width="100%"]
....
{"reg":[{"bits":7,"name": 0x33,"type":2},{"bits":5,"name": "xd","type":4},{"bits":3,"name": 0x4,"type":2},{"bits":5,"name": "xs1","type":4},{"bits":5,"name": "xs2","type":4},{"bits":7,"name": 0x14,"type":2}]}
....

Description::
The xperm8 instruction operates on bytes. The xs1 register contains a vector of XLEN/8 8-bit
elements. The xs2 register contains a vector of XLEN/8 8-bit indexes. The result is each element in
xs2 replaced by the indexed element in xs1, or zero if the index into xs2 is out of bounds.


Decode Variables::
[width="100%", cols="1,2", options="header"]
|===
|Variable Name |Location
|xs2 |$encoding[24:20]
|xs1 |$encoding[19:15]
|xd |$encoding[11:7]
|===

| *Zbkx*

|===




[#udb:doc:inst:rv32:zext_h]
=== pass:[<a id="insn:zext.h"></a>]zext.h

Synopsis::
Zero-extend halfword

Base::

[cols="1,1"]
|===
| RV32 | RV64

| &#x2713;
| &#x2713;
|===

Assembly::
zext.h xd, xs1

Encoding::
[NOTE]
This instruction has different encodings in RV32 and RV64

RV32::
[wavedrom, ,svg,subs='attributes',width="100%"]
....
{"reg":[{"bits":7,"name": 0x33,"type":2},{"bits":5,"name": "xd","type":4},{"bits":3,"name": 0x4,"type":2},{"bits":5,"name": "xs1","type":4},{"bits":12,"name": 0x80,"type":2}]}
....

RV64::
[wavedrom, ,svg,subs='attributes',width="100%"]
....
{"reg":[{"bits":7,"name": 0x3b,"type":2},{"bits":5,"name": "xd","type":4},{"bits":3,"name": 0x4,"type":2},{"bits":5,"name": "xs1","type":4},{"bits":12,"name": 0x80,"type":2}]}
....

Description::
Zero-extends the least-significant halfword of the source to XLEN by inserting
0's into all of the bits more significant than 15.

[NOTE]
The *zext.h* instruction is a pseudo-op for xref:insts:pack.adoc#udb:doc:inst:pack[pack] when ext:Zbkb[] is implemented and XLEN == 32.

[NOTE]
The *zext.h* instruction is a pseudo-op for xref:insts:packw.adoc#udb:doc:inst:packw[packw] when ext:Zbkb[] is implemented and XLEN == 64.


Decode Variables::
*RV32:*

[width="100%", cols="1,2", options="header"]
|===
|Variable Name |Location
|xs1 |$encoding[19:15]
|xd |$encoding[11:7]
|===

*RV64:*

[width="100%", cols="1,2", options="header"]
|===
|Variable Name |Location
|xs1 |$encoding[19:15]
|xd |$encoding[11:7]
|===

Included in::


[width="100%",cols="1,2",options="header"]
|===
| Extension | Version

| *Zbb*
| any
|===


Additionally, this instruction requires the following:

!ext:Zbkb[]


[#udb:doc:inst:rv32:zip]
=== pass:[<a id="insn:zip"></a>]zip

Synopsis::
Bit interleave

Base::

[cols="1,1"]
|===
| RV32 | RV64

| &#x2713;
|
|===

Assembly::
zip xd, xs1

Encoding::
[wavedrom, ,svg,subs='attributes',width="100%"]
....
{"reg":[{"bits":7,"name": 0x13,"type":2},{"bits":5,"name": "xd","type":4},{"bits":3,"name": 0x1,"type":2},{"bits":5,"name": "xs1","type":4},{"bits":12,"name": 0x8f,"type":2}]}
....

Description::
Scatters all of the odd and even bits of a source word into the high and low halves
of a destination word. It is the inverse of the unzip instruction. This instruction is available only on
RV32.


Decode Variables::
[width="100%", cols="1,2", options="header"]
|===
|Variable Name |Location
|xs1 |$encoding[19:15]
|xd |$encoding[11:7]
|===

Included in::


[width="100%",cols="1,2",options="header"]
|===
| Extension | Version

| *Zbkb*
| any
|===


Additionally, this instruction requires the following:

xlen+++()+++ == 32
